crypto.notes: Crypto trades 24/7. Use London/NY killzones for optimal entries. Current rules (Tone Vays, Van de Poppe) designed for this.

stocks.notes: Equity market hours only. Check for upcoming earnings before entering — earnings can invalidate technical setups. Gap risk on overnight holds.

futures.notes: Use RTH (regular trading hours) data for key levels. Reduce risk to 0.5-1% per trade (higher leverage). Avoid holding into expiration week. The ! suffix = continuous front-month contract.

asset_class_notes.crypto: 24/7 market. BTC dominance and ETH/BTC ratio matter. Killzone timing still applies for optimal fills.
asset_class_notes.stocks: Regular hours 0930-1600 ET. Earnings dates can invalidate technical setups. Avoid holding through earnings unless intentional. Gap risk on overnight holds.
asset_class_notes.futures: Nearly 24h session. Use RTH data for key levels. Margin requirements higher — risk 0.5-1% per trade instead of 1-2%. Roll dates matter — avoid holding into expiration week. Settlement prices are key reference levels.

strategy.name: van de Poppe + Tone Vays BTC Strategy
sources: Michaël van de Poppe (@CryptoMichNL) — EMA 21/50/200 pullback system
Tone Vays (@ToneVays) — MACD divergence + RSI confirmation
ICT (Inner Circle Trader) — order blocks, fair value gaps, killzones

indicators.ema_21: Short-term trend. Price bouncing off this = healthy uptrend.
indicators.ema_50: Medium-term trend. EMA 21 above EMA 50 = bullish alignment.
indicators.ema_200: Macro trend filter. Only take longs above this. Only take shorts below.
indicators.ema_20w: Weekly macro filter. Van de Poppe uses 20W EMA as ultimate bull/bear divider. Double tap = potential support.
indicators.rsi_14: Momentum filter. Pullback entry zone: 35-58. Overbought: >70. Oversold: <30.
indicators.macd_12_26_9: Tone Vays momentum confirmation. Line above signal = bullish. Divergence = reversal warning.
indicators.vwap: Institutional reference price. CryptoCred/TraderSZ anchor from session/week open.
indicators.volume: Breakout confirmation. Require above-average volume on breakout candles.

timeframe_structure.weekly: Van de Poppe: 21 EMA on weekly is THE key BTC trend filter. 200 EMA on weekly = ultimate bull/bear divider
timeframe_structure.daily: PRIMARY decision timeframe (Tone Vays). All MACD/RSI/EMA signals generated here. Determine macro bias
timeframe_structure.4H: Identify key levels — EMA confluence, Fibonacci zones, volume profile levels, VWAP
timeframe_structure.1H: Confirm setup direction — EMA alignment, RSI divergences
timeframe_structure.15m: Execute trades — entry patterns, killzone timing, rejection candles. Do NOT use below 1H for swing trades (Van de Poppe)

tone_vays_macd.description: Tone Vays' MACD-based BTC trading system — conservative, daily chart focused
long_entry: Price is above the 50 EMA on the Daily chart (non-negotiable filter)
MACD histogram on Daily is flipping from negative to positive (or positive and expanding)
21 EMA has crossed above or is above the 50 EMA (EMA stacking confirmation)
Strongest signal: full EMA stack (21 > 50 > 200) with MACD histogram expanding
exit_short: MACD histogram flips from positive to negative on Daily — first warning
Price closes below 21 EMA on Daily — tighten stop
Price closes below 50 EMA on Daily — hard exit
RSI dropping below 50 confirms bearish shift
tone_vays_macd.notes: Tone Vays trades BTC almost exclusively. Prefers spot or 2-3x max leverage. Very comfortable being in cash.

van_de_poppe_swing.description: Van de Poppe's Fibonacci swing setup — most replicable BTC swing trade
setup: 1. Confirm trend: BTC above 200 EMA on Daily = bullish bias
2. Wait for pullback: price retraces to 0.618-0.786 Fibonacci zone of prior swing (the 'golden pocket')
3. Confluence check: Fib zone should overlap with 21 EMA or 50 EMA, and ideally a high-volume node
4. RSI confirmation: RSI(14) on Daily near or below 40, or showing bullish divergence
5. Entry: limit order in the golden pocket (0.618-0.786 fib retracement)
6. Stop loss: below the 0.786 fib level or swing low — typically 3-5% below entry
van_de_poppe_swing.notes: Best for swing trading BTC on Daily/Weekly. Do NOT use below 1H timeframe. Scale out at each Fib extension.

bullish: MACD line is above signal line
Price is above VWAP (institutional buyers in control)
Volume is increasing on green candles (buying pressure confirmed)
bearish: MACD line is below signal line
Price is below VWAP (institutional sellers in control)
Volume is increasing on red candles (selling pressure confirmed)
neutral: Price is chopping around the EMA 200
EMA 21 and EMA 50 are flat or intertwined
MACD histogram is near zero with no clear direction
Price is near VWAP with no conviction
Volume is declining or below average

long: Macro bias is BULLISH (price > EMA 200, EMA 21 > EMA 50)
Price pulls back into the EMA 21-50 zone
RSI cools to 35-58 range (not capitulation, just a healthy pullback)
MACD line remains above signal line (momentum intact)
Enter in two tranches: 50% on first touch of zone, 50% on confirmed bounce candle
VAN DE POPPE SWING: Price in 0.618-0.786 Fib zone overlapping with 21 or 50 EMA + RSI near 40 or divergence
ICT/CRYPTOCRED: Price at confluent level (2+ of: order block, FVG, VWAP, EMA) with rejection candle on 15m
Prefer trades during London Open (02:00-05:00 EST) or NY Open (07:00-10:00 EST) killzones
short: Macro bias is BEARISH (price < EMA 200, EMA 21 < EMA 50)
Price retraces up into the EMA 21-50 zone

exit_rules: Take 33-50% profit at prior swing high or key resistance
Remaining position targets: 1.272 and 1.618 Fibonacci extensions from prior swing
Full exit if stop loss is hit — no averaging down into losers
Exit if MACD crosses bearish on the entry timeframe
TONE VAYS EXIT: MACD histogram flips against you on Daily = first warning. Close below 21 EMA = tighten stop. Close below 50 EMA = hard exit
TRAILING: Use 21 EMA on execution timeframe as trailing stop reference
Never move stop loss further from entry once placed
If price reclaims the level against you with strong volume, exit immediately
risk_rules: Risk maximum 1-2% of portfolio per trade (0.5-1% for futures due to higher leverage)
Minimum R:R of 2:1 — prefer 3:1 or better
Stop loss placed below the 0.786 Fibonacci level or most recent swing low
Position size = risk amount divided by distance to stop loss
If stop is hit, setup is invalid — do not re-enter immediately
No trading against the EMA 200 macro trend
Daily loss limit: 3% of account — stop trading for the day if hit
Weekly loss limit: 6% of account — reduce position size by 50% if hit
If 2 losing trades in a row, stop for the day
No trading in the first 15 minutes of the NY session open
No trading during low-volume weekend periods
Prefer spot or low leverage (2-3x max per Tone Vays). Being in cash is a valid position
do_not: Do not use indicator crossovers as standalone entry signals
Do not go long if price is below the EMA 200 (macro trend filter)
Do not trade without a higher-timeframe directional bias from the Daily chart
Do not average down on a losing trade
Do not move your stop loss further from entry
Do not trade if RSI is above 70 (long) or below 30 (short) — overextended, wait for pullback
Do not use Fibonacci levels as standalone signals — require confluence with EMAs or volume (Van de Poppe)
Do not use anything below 1H timeframe for swing trade decisions (Van de Poppe)

output_format.description: Morning brief must be presented in this exact format every time
output_format.bias_format: Use colored indicators: BEARISH (red), BULLISH (green), NEUTRAL (yellow)
after_table: Key takeaways: bullet list of the most important observations across all assets
Strategy recommendation: one clear paragraph on what to do based on rules
output_format.example:
| Asset | Bias | Price vs EMA 200 | Price vs 20W EMA | RSI | MACD | Signal |
| BTC | BEARISH | Below | Below | 32.8 | Bearish | Oversold, watching |

Key takeaways:
- Everything is red or neutral...

The strategy says sit on hands until assets reclaim and close above the EMA 200 on the weekly.

notes: Add macro context here — e.g. upcoming Fed meetings, halving cycle position, key on-chain levels, Bitcoin dominance trend, and anything that should affect your bias. Two primary modes: (1) Tone Vays system for daily bias and momentum confirmation via MACD + EMA stacking + RSI>50, (2) Van de Poppe system for swing entries at Fibonacci golden pocket (0.618-0.786) with EMA confluence.
